sections.paragraph:
to hide, once in Kal, Pro 22:3 כתיב, where the קרי has Niph., comp. Pro 27:12.

Niphal-

(1) to be hidden, to lie hid. Job 3:23, “to a man אֲשֶׁר דַּרְכֹּו נִסְתָּרָה to whom his way is hidden,” who does not know how to escape from calamities. Followed by מִן, καλύπτεσθαι ἀπό τινος (compare מִן No. 3, a), to lie hid from any one, Psa 38:10 Isa 40:27. Gen 31:49, “when we shall be hidden from one another,” when we shall be far from one another; followed by מֵעֵינָֽי Hos 13:14, “repentance is hid from my eyes,” i.e. I am as it were ignorant of it; followed by מִפְּנֵי (Lat. occultari a conspectu alic. Plaut.), Deu 7:20 מִלִּפְנֵי Jer 16:17 מִנֶּגֶד Amo 9:3. Part. נִסְתָּרֹות hidden things, secrets, Deu 29:28 specially hidden sins, committed ignorantly, Psa 19:13.

(2) to hide oneself, 1Sa 20:5, 19 1Sa 20:19followed by מִן Psa 55:13, and מִפְּנֵי Gen 4:14.

Piel, to hide, Isa 16:3.

Pual, to be hidden, Pro 27:5.

Hiphil הִסְתִּיר to hide. Job 3:10, וַיַּסְתֵּר עָמָל מֵעֵינָיֽ “and (that) he did (not) hide calamity from my eyes,” did not turn it away from me, avert it.-

(1) to cover over, especially the face, Exo 3:6 followed by מִן from any one, Isa 53:3, כְּמַסְתֵּר פָּנִים מִמֶּנּוּ for מ׳ פ׳ מ׳ כַּאֲשֶׁר “as one from whom they hide the face,” i.e. from whom they turn their eyes as from something disgusting and abominable (מַסְתֵּר is in this place the part. formed in the Chaldee manner, for מַסְתִּיר, which is found in four copies; it is here impers. as if כְּמֹו אֲשֶׁר מִמֶּנּוּ יַסְתִּירוּ פָנִים). Specially Jehovah is said to hide, or veil over his face-(a) when he does not regard human affairs, Psa 10:11 followed by מִן Psa 51:11, הַסְתֵּר פָּנֶיךָ מֵחֲטָאָי “hide thy face from my sins;” do not regard them, forgive them.-(b) when it denotes displeasure (opp. to הֵאִיר פָּנִיס) Psa 30:8 104:29 followed by מִן of pers. to hide the face (and turn away) from any one, Psa 22:25 27:9 88:15 102:3 119:19 Isa 54:8 64:6 , and so frequently; without פָּנִים Isa 57:17, אַכֵּהוּ הַסְתֵּר וְאֶקְצֹף “I smote them (the people) hiding my face, and being angry.”

(2) to conceal something from any one, followed by מִן of pers. 1Sa 20:2 followed by מִפְּנֵי 2Ki 11:2.

(3) to guard, to defend, Psa 31:21 27:5 followed by מִן from any one, Psa 64:3.

Hithpael הִסְתַּתֵּר to hide oneself, 1Sa 23:19 26:1 Isa 29:14 45:15 .

Derived nouns, מִסְתֹּור, מִסְתָּר, סֵתֶר, סִתְרָה, and the pr.n. סְתוּר, סִתְרִי.
